refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety plan requirements were met during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and alert operator/site manager to unsafe conditions in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide telemetry (productivity metrics, equipment state, safety status) to site manager for real-time and post-shift reporting.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate autonomous vehicle control with remote teleoperation fallback; measure productivity via onboard sensors and report to site manager dashboard.
DP-B: Implement full teleoperation (no autonomy); operator commands all vehicle motions; safety interlocks enforce worksite constraints; telemetry streamed to site manager.
DP-C: Hybrid: autonomous task planning with operator override; safety services monitor all actions; productivity measured by task completion events logged to central system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid autonomous task planning with operator override capability; safety services monitor all actions in real-time; productivity measured by task completion events (volume, tonnage, cycle time) logged to central telemetry system and reported to site manager dashboard.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle autonomy (DP) and teleoperation interface (DP-B candidate) is decoupled via operator override mechanism.
Coupling between safety services (DP) and vehicle control (DP) is managed via safety interlocks and alert channels.
Coupling between productivity measurement (DP) and telemetry infrastructure (DP) is managed via standardized Telemetry interface.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids tight coupling by using event-driven telemetry and explicit override/alert channels. Safety services operate as a monitoring/enforcement layer, not a direct control layer, reducing bidirectional dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator override latency is acceptable for safety (e.g., <500ms for critical actions).
Assumption: Autonomous task planner is sufficiently reliable to reduce operator workload without introducing new failure modes.
Risk: If autonomous planner fails, operator must be able to take full manual control; fallback path must be tested.
Risk: Productivity measurement accuracy depends on sensor calibration and task definition clarity; poor definition leads to measurement disputes.
Risk: Safety services may introduce latency in task execution if overly conservative; tuning required.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify acceptance criteria for 'representative operations': define task mix (% dig, load, transport), soil conditions, operator experience level, and duration. Define productivity_target as a range (e.g., 150–180 m³/h) to account for variability. Specify safety alert response time SLA (e.g., alert issued within 100ms of unsafe condition detected).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system boundary encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their integration. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, safety compliance, and telemetry aggregation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time), equipment state (position, fuel, temperature), safety status (alerts, compliance flags) streamed to site manager dashboard and post-shift reporting system.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site manager or autonomous planner issues task commands (dig location, load target, dump zone) to vehicle control subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Real-time safety alerts (geofence breach, operator override, unsafe condition) issued by Safety Services to operator and site manager.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation commands (operator to vehicle), telemetry (vehicle to site manager), and safety alerts (Safety Services to operator/manager).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. All sub-functions (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, telemetry) are logically contained within this module. Diffusion risk is minimal at this level; risk emerges at sub-module decomposition.

Vehicle module owns task execution (dig, load, transport) but does not own teleoperation interface, safety policy enforcement, or telemetry aggregation. Assigning productivity KPI to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for operator interface and safety compliance.

Teleoperation module owns operator interface and command transmission but does not own vehicle actuation, safety enforcement, or productivity measurement. Assigning productivity KPI to Teleoperation alone would diffuse responsibility for actual task execution and measurement.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and alert generation but does not own vehicle control, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning productivity KPI to Safety Services alone would diffuse responsibility for task execution and measurement.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The productivity KPI is a system-level outcome that depends on the integrated performance of vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry. No single sub-module can own this requirement; it must be owned at the NGHE Solution level with traceability to sub-module contributions (vehicle cycle time, teleoperation latency, safety alert response time, measurement accuracy).
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level container; decomposition to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must clearly allocate sub-requirements and interfaces to avoid ambiguity.

Risk: Productivity measurement depends on sensor accuracy and task definition; if sensors are owned by Vehicle module and task definition by Teleoperation module, measurement responsibility may be unclear.
Risk: Safety Services may constrain productivity (e.g., geofence limits, speed caps); trade-off between safety and productivity must be explicitly managed at NGHE Solution level.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: At next decomposition level, allocate sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with explicit interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Define a 'Productivity Measurement' sub-module or function within NGHE Solution to own sensor calibration, task logging, and KPI calculation. Establish a 'Safety-Productivity Trade-off Board' to manage conflicts between safety constraints and productivity targets.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid autonomous + teleoperation + safety services + telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR. The DP specifies how to achieve productivity (task planning + operator override), how to ensure safety (monitoring + alerts), and how to measure productivity (event logging + telemetry). No logical gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Productivity measurement logic may be split between Vehicle (sensor data), Teleoperation (task logging), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Mitigation: Establish a dedicated Telemetry aggregation function within NGHE Solution to centralize measurement logic.

Diffusion risk: Operator override mechanism may be implemented in both Teleoperation and Vehicle modules. Mitigation: Define override as a Teleoperation responsibility with Vehicle as a passive responder.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for this DP. All four external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are system-level concerns. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own portions of the DP but must be coordinated at the NGHE Solution level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trial. Productivity can be measured from telemetry logs (task completion events, cycle times). Safety compliance can be verified from SafetyAlert logs and worksite safety plan checklist. Operator experience and task mix must be documented to ensure representativeness.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear at the system level: NGHE Solution owns the productivity KPI and must deliver it through integrated Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Sub-allocations to individual modules will be clarified at next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Refine productivity_target definition: specify range, task mix, soil conditions, operator experience, and evaluation period duration.

Define 'representative operations' acceptance criteria: e.g., 'minimum 40 hours of operation across 5 different task types (dig, load, transport, dump, positioning) with 2 different operators on 3 different soil conditions.'
Establish safety alert response time SLA: e.g., 'SafetyAlert issued within 100ms of unsafe condition detected; operator acknowledgment required within 500ms.'
Create a Productivity Measurement specification document detailing sensor types, calibration procedures, task logging format, and KPI calculation algorithm.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that has been successfully refined into a coherent FR, mapped to a feasible DP (hybrid autonomous + teleoperation + safety services), and assigned to the correct IM boundary (NGHE Solution). The requirement is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs (vehicle task execution, teleoperation interface, safety services, telemetry) to enable detailed design and allocation to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Proceeding to next level of decomposition is justified and necessary.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and alert operator/site manager to unsafe conditions in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide telemetry (productivity metrics, equipment state, safety status) to site manager for real-time and post-shift reporting.

assumptions: Operator override latency <500ms is acceptable for safety.
Autonomous task planner is sufficiently reliable to reduce operator workload without introducing new failure modes.
Productivity measurement accuracy can be achieved via onboard sensors and task logging.
Safety Services can enforce worksite constraints without excessive latency penalty to productivity.
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and reliability for teleoperation and telemetry.
Site manager dashboard can aggregate and display telemetry in real-time.
open_risks: Autonomous planner failure mode: fallback to full manual control must be tested and validated.
Productivity measurement disputes: task definition and sensor calibration must be agreed with customer before trial.
Safety-productivity trade-off: geofence limits and speed caps may reduce productivity; tuning required.
Operator experience variability: productivity may vary significantly with operator skill; evaluation period must include representative operator pool.
Soil condition variability: productivity is highly dependent on soil type; evaluation period must include representative soil conditions.
evidence_needed: Autonomous task planner specification and reliability analysis.
Teleoperation latency and fidelity requirements (e.g., video frame rate, command response time).
Safety Services constraint specification (geofence, speed limits, alert thresholds).
Sensor specification and calibration procedure for productivity measurement.
Network Link bandwidth and reliability requirements.
Customer agreement on productivity_target, task mix, soil conditions, operator experience, and evaluation period.
